title: TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Type Recall
summary: Trace a service-status module through TypeScript 7 checking, JavaScript emission, module loading, and runtime behavior. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a compiler-to-runtime evidence notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.
Predict which TypeScript constructs erase, transform, or affect emitted JavaScript and source maps.
Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Static Runtime Boundary · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — A review build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 4.

Which action demonstrates Static Runtime Boundary without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted javascript and runtime validation.

content.text: A review build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 4.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Static Runtime Boundary condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Announcing TypeScript 7.0 — Controls native compiler behavior, parallelism, TypeScript 7 defaults, removed options, JS changes, and TS6 API compatibility.

hints: Name the Static Runtime Boundary precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A review build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 4.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A passing type check proves every runtime input is valid.

options.text: A passing type check proves every runtime input is valid.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a compiler-to-runtime evidence notebook.
